privileged_info_summary: The glycolysis energy investment phase (steps 1-5) involves six distinct carbon molecules (Glucose, Glucose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate, Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate, and Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate) processed sequentially by five enzymes (Hexokinase, Phosphoglucose Isomerase, Phosphofructokinase, Aldolase, and Triose Phosphate Isomerase). The phase consumes 2 ATP molecules and includes a critical split at Step 4 where Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate is cleaved by Aldolase into two different three-carbon molecules. All molecules and enzymes listed in task metadata are biochemically accurate and represent the standard nomenclature used in biochemistry literature.
